`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Morgan"}),": (Leaning over Sarah\u2019s shoulder, eyes fixed on the web console) Sarah, look, the egress logs are quiet now thanks to that Network Policy you slammed down, but we\u2019re flying blind in the SOC on what actually happened inside that container. If we restart that pod, the threat actors toolkit is gone forever. I need a forensic checkpoint."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Sarah"}),': (Rubbing her eyes) I hear you, Morgan. We\u2019ve got the PV snapshot done, so the disk is "frozen," but you want the full runtime state, right? The RAM, the open file descriptors, the works?']}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Morgan"}),': Exactly. In the old days, I\u2019d just dump the physical memory of the server. But here? This "container" is just a slice of a kernel. How do we pull the memory out of a running pod without killing the process?']}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Sarah"}),": I think it\u2019s called a CRIU checkpoint. It stands for Checkpoint/Restore In Userspace. Theoretically, it freezes the container and serializes its entire state into a set of image files."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Morgan"}),": (Nodding) That\u2019s the ticket. Can you run it? I want to see if they left a reverse shell sitting in the heap."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Sarah"}),": To be honest... I\u2019ve never actually done this before. OpenShift has some specific security gatekeeping for this because, well, dumping memory is a sensitive operation. Let me pull up the docs."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(e.h2,{children:"3.1 Checkpointing the breached pod"}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:["Container checkpointing was introduced as an alpha feature in Kubernetes v1.25 and graduated to beta in Kubernetes v1.30 and is a ",(0,n.jsx)(e.a,{href:"https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7008477",children:"Generally Available feature in OpenShift 4.17 onwards"}),". This functionality allows running containers to be transparently checkpointed to persistent storage and later restored to resume execution, or migrated across nodes and clusters."]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:["The content of container checkpoints can be further analyzed with the ",(0,n.jsx)(e.a,{href:"https://github.com/checkpoint-restore/checkpointctl",children:(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{children:"checkpointctl"})})," tool. This allows to perform forensic analysis in case of security incidents (e.g., suspected compromise, data exfiltration) or application failures by inspecting the saved process memory, open files, sockets, and execution context captured in the checkpoint."]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Sarah"}),": OK here we go, I need to use the kubelet API directly. I have to run a ",(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{children:"curl"})," command against the local kubelet API endpoint on the specific worker node where the ",(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{children:"payment-gateway"})," is sitting."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Morgan"}),": A curl command to dump memory? That\u2019s... surprisingly old school for such a modern platform!"]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Sarah"}),": (Laughing weakly) Welcome to Kubernetes. The command looks like this: ",(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{children:'curl -X POST "https://localhost:10250/checkpoint/payment-gateway-x/payment-gateway/httpd"'}),". I\u2019ll need to do this from a debug shell on the node itself. I think there is a helper script on a Red Hat support article I can use to make it easy."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Morgan"}),": Will the container stay alive after the checkpoint?"]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Sarah"}),`: The docs say it depends on the flags, but for a "forensic" checkpoint, the goal is to keep it running in its "frozen" state or let it continue. Since we already have the egress blocked, I\u2019ll let it keep running so we don't tip off any watchdog scripts the attacker might have left.`]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Morgan"}),": Sounds good, do it."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Sarah"}),": Initiating the node-level debug shell now. Hang on... and... started."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(e.p,{children:"Initiate a checkpoint by copying and running the following script in your OpenShift Web Terminal:"}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(e.pre,{children:(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{className:"language-bash",children:`NODE_NAME=$(oc get pods -l app=payment-gateway -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.nodeName}')
CURRENT_NS=$(oc project --short)

echo "==> Triggering fresh CRIU checkpoint on node: $NODE_NAME..."

API_RESPONSE=$(curl --silent --insecure \\
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $(oc whoami --show-token)" \\
  --request POST "$(oc whoami --show-server)/api/v1/nodes/\${NODE_NAME}/proxy/checkpoint/\${CURRENT_NS}/payment-gateway/httpd")

TARBALL_PATH=$(echo "$API_RESPONSE" | jq -r '.items[0] // empty')

if [ -z "$TARBALL_PATH" ] || [ "$TARBALL_PATH" = "null" ]; then
  echo "[!] ERROR: Checkpoint failed. API response was:"
  echo "$API_RESPONSE"
fi

CHECKPOINT_FILE=\${TARBALL_PATH##*/}

echo "==> Checkpoint created successfully: $CHECKPOINT_FILE"
echo "==> Streaming memory dump into Web Terminal..."

oc debug node/"$NODE_NAME" --to-namespace="$CURRENT_NS" -- \\
  chroot /host /bin/sh -c "cat $TARBALL_PATH" > "/tmp/$CHECKPOINT_FILE"

echo "==> Success! Verifying saved forensic artifact in /tmp/:"
ls -lah "/tmp/$CHECKPOINT_FILE"
`})}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(e.p,{children:"After a short wait you should see that a checkpoint has been created."}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(o,{children:(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.img,{src:"/workshops/static/images/fsi-security/criu-checkpoint.png",alt:"criu-checkpoint"}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(e.em,{children:"Generated criu checkpoint archive"})]})}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(e.h2,{children:"3.2 Exploring the checkpoint"}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Sarah:"})," Alright Morgan, we successfully streamed the container's memory checkpoint down to the terminal. Now we need to figure out exactly what the attacker was running."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.blockquote,{children:[`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:[(0,n.jsx)(e.strong,{children:"Morgan:"})," Perfect!  We can use the ",(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{children:"checkpointctl"})," tool to take a look at it."]}),`
`]}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:["Run the script below in your web terminal to make the ",(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{children:"checkpointctl"})," binary available."]}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(e.pre,{children:(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{className:"language-bash",children:`echo "==> Fetching precompiled checkpointctl toolkit..."
curl --silent --fail --location \\
  'https://github.com/finhennessy/workshops/raw/refs/heads/main/workshop/files/checkpointctl' \\
  --output /tmp/checkpointctl

chmod +x /tmp/checkpointctl
mkdir -p $HOME/bin
mv /tmp/checkpointctl $HOME/bin/checkpointctl
export PATH=$HOME/bin:$PATH

echo "==> Toolkit loaded successfully!"
checkpointctl

echo "--------------------------------------------------------"
echo "==> To inspect your captured memory dump, run:"
echo "checkpointctl inspect /tmp/\\$CHECKPOINT_FILE"
`})}),`
`,(0,n.jsxs)(e.p,{children:["Once ",(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{children:"checkpointctl"})," is installed in your web terminal run the command below to inspect the process tree of your checkpoint(s)."]}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(e.pre,{children:(0,n.jsx)(e.code,{className:"language-bash",children:`checkpointctl inspect --ps-tree-env /tmp/checkpoint-payment-gateway_payment-gateway*
`})}),`

`,(0,n.jsx)(e.p,{children:"If you want to dive deeper into the world of volatile memory forensics and runtime snapshots, there are fantastic open-source ecosystems to explore.
